extraction.one_line_summary: VIVIANI Elia won the bunch sprint for the second consecutive day, beating MODOLO and BENNETT after Quick-Step controlled the finale
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step deliberately asked teammates via radio whether Viviani was out of position, suggesting psychological warfare or positioning feint.
Barbin strategically sat in breakaway refusing to work, solely focused on collecting KOM points to defend jersey from Boivin.
Bahrain-Merida rode above threshold pace in peloton middle kilometers despite flat stage, unusual energy expenditure for non-sprint team.

rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: positive: held composure through tactical right-hander 200m out while others opened early, won despite barrier contact
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: neutral: third place shows consistent positioning but unable to match Viviani's late acceleration in technical finish
rider_signals.FRAPPORTI Marco: positive: attacked for breakaway KOM points showing opportunistic racing despite minimal overall reward
rider_signals.BARBIN Enrico: neutral: conservative breakaway tactics prioritizing jersey defense over stage ambition reveals limited capabilities
